rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Deployment metrics-agent is blocked from creating pods because a kubectl patch introduced privileged security context settings (runAsUser: 0, NET_ADMIN capability, allowPrivilegeEscalation: true) that violate all available OpenShift Security Context Constraints.
causalChain: Signal: SCCViolationPodBlocked — Deployment metrics-agent in demo-scc has 0 running pods (MinimumReplicasUnavailable)
Why? ReplicaSet metrics-agent-6478986598 has been unable to create any pods since 16:40:08Z, with 27 FailedCreate events
Why? Every OpenShift SCC (restricted-v2, restricted-v3, anyuid, privileged, etc.) rejects the pod spec as forbidden or non-compliant
Why? The container securityContext specifies runAsUser: 0 (root), capabilities.add: [NET_ADMIN], and allowPrivilegeEscalation: true — all of which violate cluster SCC policies

Root Cause: A kubectl patch at 2026-05-04T16:40:08Z modified the Deployment's container securityContext to introduce privileged settings, overriding the previously compliant configuration and triggering a rollout to a non-schedulable ReplicaSet

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Deployment/metrics-agent because its container spec (securityContext) is the direct source of the non-compliant configuration. Changing the Deployment's securityContext fields (runAsUser, capabilities.add, allowPrivilegeEscalation) will fix the pod scheduling failure. The ReplicaSet is a downstream symptom reporter, not the configuration owner.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Deployment affected. The fix is targeted: update three fields in the container securityContext. No other workloads in the namespace are affected (no other pods exist).
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Checked: Deployment spec and status, ReplicaSet spec and status, namespace events, all ServiceAccounts in namespace. Could not list cluster-scoped SCCs (permission denied) — mitigated by SCC error messages in ReplicaSet condition. No pods exist to inspect logs. Upstream/downstream dependencies (services, ingress) not checked as the issue is purely at pod admission.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: signal → pod creation failure → SCC rejection → specific security context violations → kubectl patch that introduced the violations. The patch timestamp and fields are confirmed via managedFields in the Deployment spec. Cannot go deeper without access to who/what triggered the kubectl patch.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool output: Deployment describe shows the exact security context values; ReplicaSet status shows 27 FailedCreate events with full SCC rejection messages; namespace events confirm the rollout timeline; managedFields confirms the kubectl patch at 16:40:08Z. Listing cluster-scoped SCCs was forbidden (permission gap) — mitigated by the detailed SCC rejection messages in the ReplicaSet condition which enumerate all available SCCs.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for this target (tier1 and tier2 remediation history both empty). N/A — no previously failed remediations to compare against.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Missing SCC binding for the service account — ruled out as secondary; the primary fix is correcting the security context since the previous config ran without custom SCC bindings. (2) Image pull failure — ruled out: prior RS pulled the same image successfully. (3) Node-level issue — ruled out: no pods are even attempted on any node; failure is at admission. (4) RBAC preventing pod creation — ruled out: error is specifically SCC validation, not RBAC.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.95: Starting at 1.0. -0.03 for inability to list cluster-scoped SCCs (mitigated by detailed error messages in RS condition). -0.02 for not being able to identify who/what triggered the kubectl patch (not critical for RCA but limits attribution). Evidence for the three specific violations and their SCC rejections is conclusive.

contributingFactors: Container runAsUser set to 0 (root), rejected by restricted-v2 (allowed: 1001090000–1001099999) and restricted-v3 (allowed: 1000–65534)
NET_ADMIN capability added to container — not permitted by any accessible SCC
allowPrivilegeEscalation: true — explicitly forbidden by all restricted SCCs
Pod-level securityContext.hostUsers is null — must be false per restricted-v3
Service account 'default' has no SCC binding granting privileged access (anyuid, privileged, etc. are all Forbidden)
A kubectl patch at 2026-05-04T16:40:08Z introduced the breaking security context changes, triggering a new ReplicaSet rollout that cannot schedule any pods
Old ReplicaSet metrics-agent-87889fdcd was already scaled down before the new RS failure was detected, resulting in full workload outage

analysis.rootCauseSummary: Deployment metrics-agent is blocked from creating pods because a kubectl patch introduced privileged security context settings (runAsUser: 0, NET_ADMIN capability, allowPrivilegeEscalation: true) that violate all available OpenShift Security Context Constraints.

## Event Summary: `demo-scc` Namespace — `metrics-agent` Deployment

### 🔴 Critical Issue: SCC Violation — ReplicaSet `metrics-agent-6478986598`

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Event Type** | Warning / FailedCreate |
| **First Seen** | 2026-05-04T16:40:08Z |
| **Last Seen** | 2026-05-04T16:51:47Z |
| **Occurrence Count** | **27 times** |
| **Involved Object** | ReplicaSet `metrics-agent-6478986598` |
| **Reporter** | `replicaset-controller` |

**Root Cause:** Pods are blocked from creation because the container's security configuration violates **all available SCCs**. Specific violations:

- `runAsUser: 0` (root) — rejected by `restricted-v2` (allowed: 1001090000–1001099999) and `restricted-v3` (allowed: 1000–65534)
- `capabilities.add: NET_ADMIN` — not permitted under any restricted SCC
- `allowPrivilegeEscalation: true` — explicitly forbidden
- `spec.securityContext.hostUsers: null` — must be set to `false` (restricted-v3)
- All named SCCs (`anyuid`, `privileged`, `hostnetwork`, `hostaccess`, etc.) — **Forbidden**: not usable by the associated user or service account

---

### 🟡 Deployment Rollout Sequence (Chronological)

| Time (UTC) | Event | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| 16:39:45 | ScalingReplicaSet ↑ | `metrics-agent-87889fdcd` scaled 0 → 1 |
| 16:39:45 | Pod Created | `metrics-agent-87889fdcd-cb4kf` created by ReplicaSet controller |
| 16:39:45 | Scheduled | Pod assigned to `dev-worker-2.redhat-internal.com` |
| 16:39:46 | Network | eth0 interface added via OVN-Kubernetes |
| 16:39:46 | Pulled | Image `registry.k8s.io/e2e-test-images/busybox:1.29-2` already present |
| 16:39:46 | Created | Container `metrics-agent` created |
| 16:39:46 | Started | Container `metrics-agent` started |
| 16:40:08 | ScalingReplicaSet ↑ | New RS `metrics-agent-6478986598` scaled 0 → 1 (rollout triggered) |
| 16:40:08 | **FailedCreate** | **SCC violations begin on new ReplicaSet** |
| 16:40:09 | Killing | Old pod `metrics-agent-87889fdcd-cb4kf` stopped |
| 16:40:09 | ScalingReplicaSet ↓ | `metrics-agent-87889fdcd` scaled 1 → 0 |
| 16:40:09 | ScalingReplicaSet ↓ | `metrics-agent-6478986598` scaled 1 → 0 |
| 16:40:13 | ScalingReplicaSet ↑ | `metrics-agent-6478986598` scaled 0 → 1 (retry) |
| 16:51:47 | **FailedCreate** | SCC failures still occurring (27th occurrence) |

---

### 🔍 Incident Assessment

**What happened:** A deployment update introduced a new ReplicaSet (`metrics-agent-6478986598`) with a security-privileged container configuration (root user, `NET_ADMIN` capability, privilege escalation). The old ReplicaSet (`metrics-agent-87889fdcd`) was successfully running but was scaled down as part of the rollout. The new ReplicaSet **cannot create any pods** due to SCC enforcement, leaving the deployment with **zero running replicas**.

**Impact:** The `metrics-agent` workload in `demo-scc` is **fully unavailable** as of 16:40:09Z.

---

### ✅ Recommended Actions

1. **Fix the container security context** in the Deployment spec:
   - Set `runAsUser` to a value within the allowed range (e.g., ≥ 1000, non-root)
   - Remove `NET_ADMIN` from `capabilities.add`
   - Set `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`
   - Set `securityContext.hostUsers: false`
2. **Grant appropriate SCC** to the service account if elevated privileges are genuinely required (e.g., bind `anyuid` or a custom SCC via RBAC)
3. **Roll back** the deployment to `metrics-agent-87889fdcd` immediately to restore service while the security context is corrected

kaResponse.analysis: Deployment metrics-agent is blocked from creating pods because a kubectl patch applied at 2026-05-04T16:40:08Z introduced privileged security context settings (runAsUser: 0, capabilities.add: [NET_ADMIN], allowPrivilegeEscalation: true) that violate all available OpenShift Security Context Constraints. The new ReplicaSet (metrics-agent-6478986598) has accumulated 27 FailedCreate events and 0 available replicas. The prior compliant ReplicaSet was already scaled down, causing a full workload outage.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Deployment metrics-agent is blocked from creating pods because a kubectl patch applied at 2026-05-04T16:40:08Z introduced privileged security context settings (runAsUser: 0, capabilities.add: [NET_ADMIN], allowPrivilegeEscalation: true) that violate all available OpenShift Security Context Constraints. The new ReplicaSet (metrics-agent-6478986598) has accumulated 27 FailedCreate events and 0 available replicas. The prior compliant ReplicaSet was already scaled down, causing a full workload outage.
causalChain: Signal: SCCViolationPodBlocked — Deployment metrics-agent in demo-scc has 0 running pods (MinimumReplicasUnavailable)
Why? ReplicaSet metrics-agent-6478986598 has been unable to create any pods since 16:40:08Z, with 27 FailedCreate events
Why? Every OpenShift SCC (restricted-v2, restricted-v3, anyuid, privileged, etc.) rejects the pod spec as forbidden or non-compliant
Why? The container securityContext specifies runAsUser: 0 (root), capabilities.add: [NET_ADMIN], and allowPrivilegeEscalation: true — all of which violate cluster SCC policies

Root Cause: A kubectl patch at 2026-05-04T16:40:08Z modified the Deployment's container securityContext to introduce privileged settings, overriding the previously compliant configuration and triggering a rollout to a non-schedulable ReplicaSet

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Deployment/metrics-agent because its container spec (securityContext) is the direct source of the non-compliant configuration. Changing the Deployment's securityContext fields (runAsUser, capabilities.add, allowPrivilegeEscalation) will fix the pod scheduling failure. The ReplicaSet is a downstream symptom reporter, not the configuration owner.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Deployment affected. The fix is targeted: update three fields in the container securityContext. No other workloads in the namespace are affected (no other pods exist).
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Checked: Deployment spec and status, ReplicaSet spec and status, namespace events, all ServiceAccounts in namespace. Could not list cluster-scoped SCCs (permission denied) — mitigated by SCC error messages in ReplicaSet condition. No pods exist to inspect logs. Upstream/downstream dependencies (services, ingress) not checked as the issue is purely at pod admission.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: signal → pod creation failure → SCC rejection → specific security context violations → kubectl patch that introduced the violations. The patch timestamp and fields are confirmed via managedFields in the Deployment spec. Cannot go deeper without access to who/what triggered the kubectl patch.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool output: Deployment describe shows the exact security context values; ReplicaSet status shows 27 FailedCreate events with full SCC rejection messages; namespace events confirm the rollout timeline; managedFields confirms the kubectl patch at 16:40:08Z. Listing cluster-scoped SCCs was forbidden (permission gap) — mitigated by the detailed SCC rejection messages in the ReplicaSet condition which enumerate all available SCCs.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for this target (tier1 and tier2 remediation history both empty). N/A — no previously failed remediations to compare against.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Missing SCC binding for the service account — ruled out as secondary; the primary fix is correcting the security context since the previous config ran without custom SCC bindings. (2) Image pull failure — ruled out: prior RS pulled the same image successfully. (3) Node-level issue — ruled out: no pods are even attempted on any node; failure is at admission. (4) RBAC preventing pod creation — ruled out: error is specifically SCC validation, not RBAC.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.95: Starting at 1.0. -0.03 for inability to list cluster-scoped SCCs (mitigated by detailed error messages in RS condition). -0.02 for not being able to identify who/what triggered the kubectl patch (not critical for RCA but limits attribution). Evidence for the three specific violations and their SCC rejections is conclusive.

contributingFactors: Container runAsUser set to 0 (root) — rejected by restricted-v2 (allowed UIDs: 1001090000–1001099999) and restricted-v3 (allowed: 1000–65534)
NET_ADMIN capability added to container — not permitted by any accessible SCC
allowPrivilegeEscalation: true — explicitly forbidden by all restricted SCCs
kubectl patch at 2026-05-04T16:40:08Z introduced the breaking security context changes, triggering a new ReplicaSet rollout
Old ReplicaSet metrics-agent-87889fdcd was scaled down before new RS failure was detected, resulting in full workload outage (0/1 replicas available)
ServiceAccount 'default' in demo-scc has no SCC binding granting privileged access (anyuid, privileged, etc. are all Forbidden)
